<!-- Code adapted from Daniel Padfield https://padpadpadpad.github.io/BayesTraitsAcrAllNodes/ -->

<!-- Arabic tree sourced from lgfam-newick (Dediu) using Glottolog topology with Grafen (1989) branch lengths. lgfam-newick repo: https://github.com/ddediu/lgfam-newick -->

<!-- Assumes:
  1. python3 is installed
  2. BayesTraits binary exists at path specified by BAYESTRAITS_PATH
  3. Grambank metadata file exists at path specified by GRAMBANK_PATH
  4. lgfam-newick repo exists at path specified by LGFAMNEWICK_PATH
-->

## 1. Setup

# Install and load packages
install.packages("here")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("ape")
install.packages("phytools")
install.packages("phangorn")
install.packages("GA")
install.packages("stringr")
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("rgriff23/btw")

library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(ape)
library(phytools)
library(phangorn)
library(GA)
library(stringr)
library(btw)

# Set where I am in the project
here::i_am('scripts/BayesTraits.Rmd')

# Set dependency path variables
BAYESTRAITS_PATH <- "../BayesTraitsV4.0.0-OSX/BayesTraitsV4"
GRAMBANK_PATH <- "../../datasets/grambank-grambank-7ae000c/cldf/StructureDataset-metadata.json"
LGFAMNEWICK_PATH <- "../../lgfam-newick"


## 2. Phylogenetic tree loading

# Load Afro-Asiatic tree from lgfam-newick (Grafen branch lengths, Glottolog topology)
lgfamnewick_code_path <- paste(
  LGFAMNEWICK_PATH,
  "/code/FamilyTrees.R",
  sep = ""
  )
source(lgfamnewick_code_path)

lgfamnewick_tree_path <- paste(
  LGFAMNEWICK_PATH, 
  "/output/glottolog/glottolog-newick-grafen.csv",
  sep = ""
  )
classif <- languageclassification(
  csv.file = lgfamnewick_tree_path,
  csv.tree.column = "Tree"
  )

afroasiatic <- classif[[which(sapply(classif, function(x) attr(x, "tree.name")) == "Afro-Asiatic")]]

# Clean labels: phytools keeps surrounding quotes; lgfam-newick uses `` for apostrophes
clean_labels <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("^'|'$", "", x) # strip surrounding single quotes
  x <- gsub(" \\[.*", "", x) # strip glottolog metadata [i-...][w-...] etc.
  x <- gsub("``", "'", x, fixed=TRUE) # unescape backtick-pairs to apostrophe
  x <- gsub(" ", "_", x)
  x
}
afroasiatic$tip.label  <- clean_labels(afroasiatic$tip.label)
afroasiatic$node.label <- clean_labels(afroasiatic$node.label)

# Extract the Arabic sub-clade
tree <- extract.clade(afroasiatic, "Arabic")

## STOPPED HERE

# Prune tree to keep only ones with Grambank feature values
# Changes recorded here: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1HY9ZQ_Vhrd_n2OKdKs_z37s2da0056ZRCeZTNWtMoaQ/edit?gid=0#gid=0
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Tunisian_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Moroccan_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Judeo-Moroccan_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Andalusian_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Algerian_Saharan_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Algerian_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Judeo-Tripolitanian_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Sudanese_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Saidi_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "North_Mesopotamian_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Judeo-Iraqi_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Qashqa-Darya_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Khorasan_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Tajiki_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Gilit_Mesopotamian_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Ru'us_al-Jibal")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Dhofari_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Omani_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Najdi_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Judeo-Yemeni_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Ta'izzi-Adeni_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Hijazi_Arabic")
tree = drop.tip(tree, "Hadrami_Arabic")

# Rename "Levantine_Arabic" to "North_Levantine_Arabic" to match Grambank
tree$tip.label[10] <- "North_Levantine_Arabic"

# Add branch lengths
branch_lengths <- rep(0.2, 23)
tree$edge.length <- branch_lengths

# Plot tree
tree_plot <- plot(tree, show.node.label = TRUE, root.edge = TRUE)


## 3. Trait data loading

# Run script to generate trait file
# Replace 107 with Grambank parameter number in question
system("python3 generate_trait_file.py ../../datasets/grambank-grambank-7ae000c/cldf/StructureDataset-metadata.json GB111")

# Read in trait data
trait <- read.table(here('data/trait.txt'), col.names=c("language", "value"))

# Check that the trait table Language column order is the same as the tree tip order
sum(trait$language == tree$tip.label) == nrow(trait)


## 4. BayesTraits input formatting

# Convert tree to a dataframe
tree_data <- as_tibble(tree)

# For each internal node, we want to get the labels of its offspring nodes
# Internal nodes themselves do not have labels in this tree

# Create a dataframe of internal nodes with a new empty column for offspring tip labels
d_nodes <- tibble(nodes = dplyr::filter(tree_data, is.na(label)) %>% pull(node), label = NA)

# Calculate offspring tip labels for each node and fill in label column
for (i in 1:nrow(d_nodes)) {
  temp_node = d_nodes$nodes[i]
  temp_offspring <- offspring(tree_data, temp_node, tiponly = TRUE)
  d_nodes$label[i] <- temp_offspring$label %>% paste0(collapse = ' ')
  }

# Add in the correct commands needed to reconstruct the ancestral states 
d_nodes <- mutate(
  d_nodes, 
  first_command = paste('AddTag', paste('T', nodes, sep = ''), label, sep = ' '), 
  second_command = paste('AddNode', nodes, paste('T', nodes, sep = ''), sep = ' ')
  )

# Output this as a vector
to_save <- select(d_nodes, first_command, second_command) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), values_to = 'command') %>%
  pull(command)

# Format and save to control file that will be input to BayesTraits
# Choose 1, 1 -> MultiState evolution model and Maximum Likelihood analysis method
to_save <- append("1", to_save)
to_save <- append("1", to_save)
to_save <- append(to_save, "LogFile ../data/ML")
to_save <- append(to_save, "Run")
write.table(to_save, here('scripts/max_likelihood_control.txt'), quote=FALSE, col.names = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

# Save tree as nexus file as required by BayesTraits
write.nexus(tree, file = here('data/tree.nex'))


## 4. Run maximum likelihood analysis in BayesTraits

# This needs to be done directly using BayesTraits in the command line because although rgriff23/btw is an R wrapper package for BayesTraits, it does not support versions beyond V3. It is used further below to parse the output log file however

system("../BayesTraitsV4.0.0-OSX/BayesTraitsV4 ../data/tree.nex ../data/trait.txt < max_likelihood_control.txt")


## 5. BayesTraits output reading

# Read in output from BayesTraits
output <- parse_log(here('data/ML.Log.txt'))

# Grab just the nodes and transform into a table with the probability of
# each state for each node
d_output <- select(output$results, starts_with('X')) %>% 
   pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = 'var', values_to = 'prob') %>% 
   filter(!is.na(prob)) %>% 
   separate(col = var, c('node', 'p', 'state'), sep = '\\.') %>% 
   select(-p) %>% 
   mutate(node = gsub('X', '', node))

# Transform into table with two columns for each state probability per node
d_wide <- d_output %>%
  mutate(node = as.numeric(node)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
  names_from = state,
  values_from = prob,
  names_prefix = "prob_"
  )

# Plot tree with trait values and state probabilities
ggtree(tree) %<+% d_wide %<+% rename(trait, label = language) +
  geom_tiplab(size = 3.5) +
  geom_tippoint(aes(fill = factor(value)), shape = 21, size = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "white", "1" = "black"), name = "Trait") +
  geom_nodepoint(aes(color = prob_1)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  labs(color = "P(1)") +
  geom_nodelab(aes(label = paste0("P(0)=", round(prob_0, 2),
                                  "\n",
                                  "P(1)=", round(prob_1, 2))),
              size = 3.5)
